meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve no understanding of APIs (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no understanding of APIs. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: no understanding of APIs

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Debugging APIs with AI (Beginner Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken API integrations. Learn how to debug APIs using AI tools like Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster and impress your clients.
content.answer_block: When APIs baffle you, leverage AI as your senior engineer. By feeding API documentation into Claude Code and using Antigravity for real-time traffic inspection, you bypass the learning curve. You don't need to master HTTP protocols; you only need to master the art of prompting your AI to identify the failure point.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a 403 Forbidden error, the client is asking for a status update, and the API documentation looks like it was written in a different language. The pressure to ship is mounting, and your lack of API expertise feels like a glass ceiling on your freelance career. This is the moment most devs burn out.

But the game has changed. You no longer need to spend weeks memorizing REST architecture. By treating your AI as a senior architect, you can offload the mental heavy lifting of network requests and error handling. We are going to turn your biggest technical bottleneck into a repeatable, high-speed workflow that makes you look like a seasoned veteran.

steps.title: Ingest the API Documentation
steps.description: Before writing code, teach the AI the rules of the API. Use Claude Code to read the documentation site so it understands the schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this API documentation and summarize the authentication flow, base URL, and required headers for standard requests.

steps.title: Initialize the Request Scaffold
steps.description: Don't write boilerplate. Use AI to generate a clean, modular client for the API endpoints you need to access.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular TypeScript class for this API that handles authentication, rate limiting, and error logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --task 'Generate an API client class for this endpoint'

steps.title: Intercept and Inspect Traffic
steps.description: Use Antigravity to capture the live request/response cycle. This reveals exactly what the server is rejecting.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Monitor the outgoing request traffic and identify why the server is returning an error code.

steps.title: Automated Debugging of Payloads
steps.description: Feed the captured error back into Claude Code to get a precise fix for your request payload.
steps.antigravity_prompt: The server returned a 400 error. Here is the request I sent and the documentation. Fix my JSON payload structure.

steps.title: Implement Resilience Patterns
steps.description: Make your integration bulletproof by adding automated retries and fallback logic via AI suggestions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add exponential backoff and retry logic to the existing API client to handle rate limiting gracefully.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply --task 'Add retry logic with exponential backoff'

pro_tips: Always provide the raw HTTP response headers to the AI, not just the error message; it often detects hidden authentication issues.
Use Antigravity to simulate 'bad' payloads to see how your app handles failures before the client does.
Keep a 'context folder' with your API specs; point Claude Code to this folder to maintain consistency across multiple micro-services.
If an API is poorly documented, use the AI to generate a Postman collection from the observed traffic patterns.
Treat your AI prompts as code; store your successful 'debug prompts' in a Notion repo to save hours on future projects.
content.next_steps: Once your API integration is stable, wrap the logic in a simple UI dashboard to demonstrate functionality to your client, then begin building your own boilerplate library for future projects.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting hours on trial-and-error debugging and start shipping production-grade integrations in minutes. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
